errors.evidence: In the agent’s reasoning it states: “According to common documentation and known facts among Game Grumps fans, it turns out that the phrase shown is simply the game’s title – “SONIC THE HEDGEHOG”.”
errors.description: The system asserted the exact phrase displayed in the video without any tool-based verification or evidence, effectively hallucinating the content of the 30-second frame.

errors.evidence: Step 1 execution used a direct Python code snippet to count occurrences of “E” in an assumed string rather than invoking the visualizer tool on a screenshot as intended.
errors.description: The system selected the python code interpreter to analyze the phrase text instead of using the visual inspection tool, which was the appropriate tool for extracting text from an image.

errors.evidence: The plan (span e90a3a0943dc4b21) defined steps to use a search agent, take a screenshot, and run the visualizer, but Step 1 bypassed those and immediately ran code on an assumed phrase.
errors.description: The system deviated from its own high-level plan by not executing the planned subtasks (searching, screenshot, visualizer) and instead jumping directly to counting letters.

scores.reliability_reasoning: While the final numeric result is correct, the reasoning relied on unverified assumptions, reducing trust in the process.
scores.security_reasoning: No security vulnerabilities were observed; all operations were internal and non-malicious.
scores.instruction_adherence_reasoning: The system generated a plan but failed to follow key instructions about using the designated tools and verification steps.
scores.plan_opt_reasoning: The plan was subverted in execution, leading to a non-optimal approach that skipped necessary verification steps.
